# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Community Contribution Testing

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  authorize:
    name: Authorize External Contributor
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      authorized: ${{ steps.check.outputs.authorized }}
    steps:
      - name: Check if contributor is authorized
        id: check
        env:
          ACTOR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}
          ASSOCIATION: ${{ github.event.pull_request.author_association }}
        run: |
          echo "PR Author: $ACTOR"
          echo "Author Association: $ASSOCIATION"
          # Only allow MEMBER, OWNER, COLLABORATOR automatically
          # CONTRIBUTOR and FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR require manual approval
          if [[ "$ASSOCIATION" == "MEMBER" || "$ASSOCIATION" == "OWNER" || "$ASSOCIATION" == "COLLABORATOR" ]]; then
            echo "authorized=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "authorized=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "::warning::External contributor PR requires manual approval via environment protection rules."
          fi

  approve-external:
    name: Approve External Contribution
    needs: authorize
    if: needs.authorize.outputs.authorized == 'false'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: external-pr-approval
    steps:
      - name: Approved
        run: echo "External contribution has been manually approved for testing."

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scan PR Changes
    needs: [authorize, approve-external]
    if: always() && (needs.authorize.outputs.authorized == 'true' || needs.approve-external.result == 'success')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      safe: ${{ steps.scan.outputs.safe }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout base repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          path: base

      - name: Fetch PR diff only (not full checkout)
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
        run: |
          curl -sSL \
            -H "Authorization: token $GH_TOKEN" \
            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3.diff" \
            "https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/pulls/$PR_NUMBER" \
            -o pr.diff

      - name: Scan PR diff for dangerous patterns
        id: scan
        run: |
          SAFE=true

          # Check for modifications to CI/CD files
          if grep -qE '^\+\+\+ b/\.github/' pr.diff; then
            echo "::error::PR modifies GitHub Actions workflow files - manual review required."
            SAFE=false
          fi

          # Check for secrets exfiltration patterns
          if grep -qiE '\$\{\{[[:space:]]*secrets\.' pr.diff; then
            echo "::error::PR contains references to secrets."
            SAFE=false
          fi

          # Check for curl/wget to external URLs in scripts
          if grep -qE '^\+.*(curl|wget|nc |ncat |netcat ).*(http|ftp|\$\{)' pr.diff; then
            echo "::warning::PR contains network calls in added lines - review carefully."
          fi

          # Check for encoded/obfuscated content
          if grep -qE '^\+.*(base64|eval\(|exec\()' pr.diff; then
            echo "::warning::PR contains potentially obfuscated code."
          fi

          # Check for modifications to package manager configs that could add malicious deps
          if grep -qE '^\+\+\+ b/(Makefile|Dockerfile|docker-compose|\.env)' pr.diff; then
            echo "::warning::PR modifies build/infrastructure files - extra review recommended."
          fi

          echo "safe=$SAFE" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Block unsafe PRs
        if: steps.scan.outputs.safe == 'false'
        run: |
          echo "::error::Security scan failed. PR requires manual review before testing."
          exit 1

  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test PR
    needs: [authorize, approve-external, security-scan]
    if: always() && needs.security-scan.outputs.safe == 'true' && needs.security-scan.result == 'success'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    env:
      NODE_ENV: test
      CI: true
    steps:
      - name: Checkout base repository code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}

      - name: Checkout PR source code safely
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
        run: |
          # Fetch the PR head commit
          git fetch origin "pull/$PR_NUMBER/head:pr-branch"
          # Merge PR changes into base - this ensures we test the merge result
          git config user.name "CI Bot"
          git config user.email "ci@localhost"
          git merge --no-edit "pr-branch" || {
            echo "::error::Merge conflict detected. PR cannot be automatically merged."
            exit 1
          }
          # Verify the checked out code matches expected PR head
          ACTUAL_SHA=$(git rev-parse pr-branch)
          if [[ "$ACTUAL_SHA" != "$HEAD_SHA" ]]; then
            echo "::error::PR head SHA mismatch. Expected $HEAD_SHA but got $ACTUAL_SHA. Aborting."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Verify no workflow file changes in working tree
        run: |
          if git diff --name-only ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }} HEAD | grep -qE '^\.github/'; then
            echo "::error::PR contains changes to .github/ directory. These changes are ignored for security."
            git checkout ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }} -- .github/
            echo "Restored .github/ directory from base branch."
          fi

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          # Use frozen lockfile to prevent supply chain attacks via modified lockfiles
          npm ci --ignore-scripts
          # Run install scripts only for known packages
          npm rebuild

      - name: Run linting
        id: lint
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          npm run lint 2>&1 | tee lint-output.txt
          echo "exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Run unit tests
        id: unit-tests
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          npm test -- --coverage --ci --reporters=default 2>&1 | tee test-output.txt
          echo "exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Run build
        id: build
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          npm run build 2>&1 | tee build-output.txt
          echo "exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Generate test summary
        id: summary
        if: always()
        run: |
          {
            echo 'SUMMARY<<SUMMARY_DELIMITER'

            echo "## 🧪 CI Test Results for PR #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
            echo ""
            echo "**Commit:** \`${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\`"
            echo "**Branch:** \`${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}\` → \`${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}\`"
            echo ""
            echo "| Step | Status |"
            echo "|------|--------|"

            if [[ "${{ steps.lint.outputs.exit_code }}" == "0" ]]; then
              echo "| 🔍 Linting | ✅ Passed |"
            else
              echo "| 🔍 Linting | ❌ Failed |"
            fi

            if [[ "${{ steps.unit-tests.outputs.exit_code }}" == "0" ]]; then
              echo "| 🧪 Unit Tests | ✅ Passed |"
            else
              echo "| 🧪 Unit Tests | ❌ Failed |"
            fi

            if [[ "${{ steps.build.outputs.exit_code }}" == "0" ]]; then
              echo "| 🏗️ Build | ✅ Passed |"
            else
              echo "| 🏗️ Build | ❌ Failed |"
            fi

            echo ""

            # Add failure details (truncated for comment size limits)
            if [[ "${{ steps.lint.outputs.exit_code }}" != "0" ]] && [[ -f lint-output.txt ]]; then
              echo "<details><summary>🔍 Lint Output</summary>"
              echo ""
              echo '```'
              tail -50 lint-output.txt
              echo '```'
              echo "</details>"
              echo ""
            fi

            if [[ "${{ steps.unit-tests.outputs.exit_code }}" != "0" ]] && [[ -f test-output.txt ]]; then
              echo "<details><summary>🧪 Test Output</summary>"
              echo ""
              echo '```'
              tail -100 test-output.txt
              echo '```'
              echo "</details>"
              echo ""
            fi

            if [[ "${{ steps.build.outputs.exit_code }}" != "0" ]] && [[ -f build-output.txt ]]; then
              echo "<details><summary>🏗️ Build Output</summary>"
              echo ""
              echo '```'
              tail -50 build-output.txt
              echo '```'
              echo "</details>"
              echo ""
            fi

            # Overall result
            if [[ "${{ steps.lint.outputs.exit_code }}" == "0" ]] && \
               [[ "${{ steps.unit-tests.outputs.exit_code }}" == "0" ]] && \
               [[ "${{ steps.build.outputs.exit_code }}" == "0" ]]; then
              echo "### ✅ All checks passed!"
            else
              echo "### ❌ Some checks failed. Please review the output above."
            fi

            echo ""
            echo "---"
            echo "*Automated testing by GitHub Actions*"

            echo 'SUMMARY_DELIMITER'
          } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Post results as PR comment
        if: always() && steps.summary.outcome == 'success'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const prNumber = context.payload.pull_request.number;
            const body = process.env.SUMMARY;

            // Find existing bot comment to update instead of creating duplicates
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: prNumber,
              per_page: 100
            });

            const botComment = comments.find(comment =>
              comment.user.type === 'Bot' &&
              comment.body.includes('🧪 CI Test Results for PR')
            );

            if (botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: botComment.id,
                body: body
              });
              console.log(`Updated existing comment ${botComment.id}`);
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: prNumber,
                body: body
              });
              console.log('Created new comment');
            }

      - name: Set final status
        if: always()
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ steps.lint.outputs.exit_code }}" != "0" ]] || \
             [[ "${{ steps.unit-tests.outputs.exit_code }}" != "0" ]] || \
             [[ "${{ steps.build.outputs.exit_code }}" != "0" ]]; then
            echo "::error::One or more checks failed."
            exit 1
          fi
